summary: Jeff Atwood explores the concept of 'sharpening the saw' - the practice of improving as a programmer through activities beyond just writing code. He argues that while doing the work is essential, being too heads-down to reflect, discuss, or study actually impedes progress. The post recommends reading programming blogs with an open mind, looking for even one valuable insight rather than dismissing content that challenges your views. Atwood highlights Hacker News and programming reddit as valuable link aggregation sites for discovering quality content, while cautioning that excessive saw-sharpening can become procrastination.
key_points: The best way to improve is practice, but pure heads-down coding without reflection or study prevents real progress
Reading blogs effectively means looking for just one valuable insight rather than dismissing everything that challenges your worldview
Hacker News and programming reddit serve as excellent curated sources for programming content worth reading
A developer completely disinterested in self-improvement is a major red flag
Obsession with improvement can be healthy - it reveals natural motivation and drive
money_quotes: I am too busy sawing to take time to sharpen my saw.
You have to strike a mindful balance between practicing your craft and thinking about how you practice your craft.
We win if we can find just one thing in there that makes us better programmers.
It's a huge win if we read one hundred posts and learn one new valuable thing.
A developer who seems completely disinterested in it at all is a huge red flag.
People are often successful not despite their dysfunctions but because of them. Obsessions are one of the greatest telltale signs of success.
key_insight: Balance doing the work with thinking about how you do the work - even finding one valuable insight from a hundred blog posts is a huge win for your growth as a programmer.
